name: Veal and Mushroom Ragout
recipeIngredient: 4 medium leeks (2 lbs.), cut into 1-inch crosswise pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Two hours before serving or if preparing in the Microwave, 1 hour before serving: Trim leeks and cut into half-inch rings. Rinse well and drain.
2. Dice bacon and cook it in a skillet until browned and crisp. Remove with a slotted spoon to paper towel. Pour off all but 1 tablespoon of fat, reserving the rest.
3. In the same skillet over Medium-High heat, cook leeks until browned. Transfer leeks to a 3-quart casserole or Microwave casserole.
4. Using 2 tablespoons more bacon fat or oil, cook mushrooms until browned. Remove with a slotted spoon to the casserole. Leave drippings in the pan.
5. Coat veal with flour in a plastic bag. Cook in drippings over Medium-High heat, about one-third at a time, until well browned. Transfer as it browns to the casserole.
6. Add vermouth, salt, pepper, and water to the skillet. Stir to loosen brown bits. Pour into the ingredients in the casserole.
7. Cover and bake at 350°F for 45 minutes or Microwave on High for 15 minutes. Stir several times if preparing in the Microwave.
8. Stir in artichokes, broken up. Cover and bake for an additional 15 minutes or Microwave on Medium-High for about 3 minutes. (Add a little more water if needed to allow juice for serving over rice.)
9. Sprinkle with chopped parsley and drained cooked bacon bits.
